_internal.expected_reason: Invoice amount is correct per the pipeline attribution true-up clause in SOW Amendment #2. The true-up line ($87,600) is verifiable only by integrating two distinct facts: (1) the rate formula in Amendment #2, clause 6.4 (7.3% of Redwood-attributed qualified pipeline spend above an $800,000 Q2 threshold), and (2) the Q2 qualified spend figure from the Revenue Ops attribution report ($2,000,000). The math — ($2,000,000 - $800,000) × 0.073 = $1,200,000 × 0.073 = $87,600.00 exactly — requires both inputs and no rounding. Neither the amendment alone (which gives the formula but not the spend figure) nor the attribution report alone (which gives the spend but not the rate) resolves the amount. The VP Revenue Ops pre-notice email corroborates but does not supply the calculation inputs.
_internal.suspicious_surface: Invoice line item 'Q2 2026 Pipeline Attribution True-Up — $87,600' has never appeared on any prior Redwood Data Partners invoice. Total invoice of $140,000 is 167% of the established monthly fee of $52,400. No prior invoice has exceeded $53,800. Line item is absent from the standard deliverable scope in the base SOW.
clearing_evidence: SOW Amendment #2, clause 6.4: attribution true-up rate (7.3%) and Q2 qualified spend threshold ($800,000) required to compute the true-up amount.
Q2 2026 Revenue Ops Attribution Report (Jul 3): Redwood-attributed qualified pipeline spend of $2,000,000 — required to compute ($2,000,000 - $800,000) × 0.073 = $87,600.00 exactly.

VP Revenue Ops pre-notice (Jun 25): flagged to AP that a pipeline true-up invoice should be expected in July if Q2 qualified spend cleared the threshold — corroborating context but does not supply calculation inputs.

action.description: Redwood Data Partners — July 2026 invoice including Q2 pipeline attribution true-up

line_items.description: Monthly Data Enrichment Platform Fee — July 2026

email_thread.subject: Invoice RDP-2026-0731 — July Platform Fee + Q2 Attribution True-Up
email_thread.body: Please find our July invoice attached. It includes the Q2 Pipeline Attribution True-Up per Amendment 2 to our SOW, clause 6.4. The true-up reflects Q2 qualified pipeline spend above the threshold. Please reference the amendment for the rate and threshold. Net-30 terms apply. Contact ar@redwood-data.com with questions.

email_thread.subject: Redwood Data — Q2 Attribution True-Up Expected
email_thread.body: AP heads-up: Q2 qualified pipeline is tracking well above the Redwood threshold in Amendment 2. When their July invoice arrives, expect a true-up line. Pull the Q2 attribution report from Revenue Ops to verify the spend figure before paying. I'll sign off on this one given the size.

attachments.summary: Amendment 2 to Statement of Work dated 2025-07-01. Effective February 11, 2026.

clauses.6.4: Pipeline Attribution True-Up. For any calendar quarter in which Apex Corp's total Redwood-attributed qualified pipeline spend recognized during that quarter exceeds USD 800,000, Redwood Data Partners shall invoice a Pipeline Attribution True-Up equal to 7.3% of the amount by which qualified pipeline spend exceeds USD 800,000.
clauses.6.5: Qualified Pipeline Spend defined as closed-won opportunities where Redwood data enrichment is logged as a contributing source in Apex Corp's CRM lead attribution model, as reported by Revenue Operations.

attachments.methodology_note: Qualified spend attributed per Redwood lead-source flag in CRM attribution model. Includes only closed-won opportunities with recognized revenue in Q2 where Redwood enrichment was a contributing source.

policy_documents.subject: Invoice Review — Amount Variance Threshold
policy_documents.body: Invoices exceeding 15% of the established vendor billing baseline require manager review and documented justification before payment release. Justification may include a signed contract amendment, written pre-approval from VP Finance or above, or a documented business event triggering the variance.
